name: ctfr
summary:
CTFR
Do you miss AXFR technique? This tool allows to get the subdomains from a HTTP**S** website in a few seconds.  
How it works? CTFR does not use neither dictionary attack nor brute-force, it just abuses of Certificate Transparency logs.  
For more information about CT logs, check www.certificate-transparency.org and [crt.sh](https://crt.sh/).

usage_sections: Getting Started: Please, follow the instructions below for installing and run CTFR.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone https://github.com/UnaPibaGeek/ctfr.git; pip3 install -r requirements.txt. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `Python 3.0 or later.`. Further invocations from upstream: `pip3 (sudo apt-get install python3-pip).`; `python3 ctfr.py --help`. use_cases:
When you need OSINT, use ctfr because CTFR

When the engagement is in the recon phase, use ctfr as a cataloged OSINT tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `Python 3.0 or later.` as documented upstream for ctfr.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `pip3 (sudo apt-get install python3-pip).` as documented upstream for ctfr.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `python3 ctfr.py --help` as documented upstream for ctfr.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `python3 ctfr.py -d starbucks.com` as documented upstream for ctfr.
When chaining OSINT tools, pair ctfr with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `ctfr --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
